content:
```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["STEGO TEXT hides messages like a STEGOsaurus hides in plain sight. Just as the dinosaur blends with its environment, steganography blends data within a carrier text.", "L S B: Look for the Least Significant Bit. Changing the last bit of a character is Like a Small Blip, invisible to the human eye but a core technique for data hiding.", "WHITE SPACE IS YOUR FRIEND: Extra spaces, tabs, and line breaks are Invisible Ink for digital messages."],
  "keyPoints": ["Steganography is the practice of concealing a message within another medium, making the very existence of the message undetectable.", "A core technique involves modifying the Least Significant Bits (LSB) of character codes or using non-printing characters and whitespace to encode information.", "The primary goal is covert communication, differing from cryptography which focuses on scrambling content to make it unreadable."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Confusing steganography (hiding the existence of data) with cryptography (scrambling data to make it unreadable). They are complementary, not the same.", ["Overlooking the carrier file's properties, leading to a hidden message that is statistically detectable or visibly alters the text's format.", "Using simple substitution methods that are easily broken or discovered by automated analysis tools."]
}
```
